one_line_ask: Review every raw concert clip from the Velvet Hour Sessions multi-cam shoot, judge keepers vs unusable takes, and deliver a structured shot-selection log (per-clip verdict, reason, best usable stretch, usability rating, coverage tag) plus a contact-sheet PDF of the flagged best-take frames.
brief: Velvet Hour Sessions is a fictional intimate live-music night run by the Velvet Hour Room, a small arts-and-culture venue that films one acoustic set a month for its social channels (Instagram, Facebook, YouTube). Last Thursday we filmed the singer-songwriter 'Marlowe Fenn' doing a four-song stripped-back set on three cameras — a locked wide, a roaming handheld, and a close-up on the guitar/hands — and the card dump gave us a folder of a dozen-ish raw clips of very mixed quality. Before our editor builds the actual cut we need a fresh, disciplined pair of eyes to do the footage-review pass first: watch every raw clip end to end, decide which takes are genuinely usable and which are junk, and hand us back a selection log we can build the timeline from. Do NOT cut, grade, add music, or produce a finished video — this is a review-and-select job, exactly the first stage of the edit. You will receive: (1) velvet_hour_raw_clips (the folder of raw concert clips — some are clean keepers, several are unusable: one badly out of focus during the second song, one with a big camera bump / lens smack, one that's mostly dead air and tuning chatter before the song starts, one near-duplicate of a b
